body.title: agent_datacenter: rack.* MCP tools — devices, health, channels
body.result: agent_datacenter/skeleton/health.py: rack_devices(registry), rack_channels(imap_server), rack_health_async(devices). Skeleton._setup_rack_tools() delegates to health.py. _health_rollup() removed.
body.description:
Implement the rack.* MCP namespace — the meta-level tools that enumerate and inspect the entire rack.

Proposed shape:
- agent_datacenter/skeleton/health.py:
  def rack_devices() -> list[dict]: reads registry, returns all device records
  def rack_health() -> dict: delegates to rack-health-rollup (T-adc-rack-health-rollup)
  def rack_channels() -> list[str]: returns all IMAP mailbox names from IMAP server
- These are registered as MCP tools under rack.* prefix in Skeleton
- rack.devices: {device_id, name, status, mailbox, registered_at}
- rack.health: {device_id: {healthy: bool, details: dict, error: str|None}}
- rack.channels: ['Shared', 'CC.0', 'igor-wild-0001', ...]

Affected files:
- ~/dev/src/agent_datacenter/agent_datacenter/skeleton/health.py

Design rules:
- oop-first: functions grouped in health.py module, not inlined in skeleton.py
- docs-in-code: each function's docstring shows the return shape

Scope boundary:
IN: rack.devices, rack.health (stub delegates), rack.channels implementations
OUT: health fan-out implementation (T-adc-rack-health-rollup)

Test plan:
- rack.devices() returns list containing at least 'skeleton'
- rack.channels() returns ['Shared'] after IMAP init (requires T-adc-imap-server-embedded gated)
- rack.health() returns dict keyed by device_id
